attacking_style: Specializes in winning from large breakaways on steep uphill finishes (14-16% gradients). Attacks in final 500m with powerful finishing kick, timing move after earlier attacks have shattered the group.
team_tactics: Operates as opportunist in large breaks rather than leading team support. Conserves energy while others (GANNA, CAICEDO, etc.) do the destructive work, then counters in final meters.
terrain_preference: Excels on brutally steep finishes (14-16%) from breakaways. Struggles on sustained 15%+ gradients when forced to follow wheels rather than finish from reduced group.
vulnerability: Gets dropped on long steep sections (15%+) when unable to dictate pace. Requires others to shatter group first—lacks sustained climbing to create selection alone.
key_patterns: Three Vuelta stage wins from large breakaways, establishing this as primary winning tactic.
Consistently attacks in final 200-500m on steep finishes, not earlier on climbs.
Wins after high-pace shattering by others (GANNA's infernal pace, CAICEDO's flamme rouge attack).
Strong on 14-16% pitches but loses contact on similar gradients when tactics don't favor.
Breakaway specialist targeting stages GC riders will concede, avoiding GC confrontation.
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta win with strong finishing kick shows maintained breakaway prowess into current seasons
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 11
